entities.name: Acrobius
entities.description: Recounts five differences of images or imaginations in sleep.

entities.name: Peucer
entities.description: Author who provides causes and occasions of dreams.

entities.name: Galen
entities.description: Physician cited regarding the physical nature of dreams through humors.

entities.name: Plato
entities.description: Attributed dreams to the forms and ingendered knowledges of the soul.

entities.name: Avicen
entities.description: Attributed dreams to the last intelligence that moves the moon.

entities.name: Aristotle
entities.description: Attributed dreams to the phantastical sense; also linked to melancholic vapors.

entities.name: Averroes
entities.description: Attributed dreams to the imaginative sense.

entities.name: Albert
entities.description: Attributed dreams to the influence of superior bodies.

entities.name: Cicero
entities.description: Confutes the vanity and folly of dream interpreters.

entities.name: Nicolaus Hemingius
entities.description: Divides diabolical dreams into three sorts.

entities.name: Salomon
entities.description: Cited regarding casual dreams and the multitude of business.

entities.name: Abraham
entities.description: Cited as the first inventor of dream exposition.

entities.name: Daniel
entities.description: Prophet known for interpreting dreams and receiving divine revelations.
